title: Infiltrator probe droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:13k.jpg]] |name=Infiltrator probe droid |creator=[[Damon Krell]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |manufacturer=[[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]/[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] technicians |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[Arakyd Probot Series]] |model= |class=[[Probe droid/Legends|Probe droid]][[Star Wars (1977) 45|''Star Wars'' (1977) 45]] |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament=2 enhanced [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] |equipment=*[[Armor/Legends|Armor]] plating *Several retractable manipulator arms *Several [[Computer/Legends|computer]] interface probes |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|That thing's tapped into every system… made the entire ship an extension of itself! Someone's ''really'' augmented its programming! Probe droids were never meant to operate this independently!|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} The '''Infiltrator probe droid''' was a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] produced in a collaboration between [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Damon Krell]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Augmented with superior [[Armor/Legends|armor]], weapons and [[Computer/Legends|computer]] interface capabilities, an Infiltrator had the ability to tap into and completely control a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Krell attempted to use an [[13-K|Infiltrator droid]] to destroy the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], only to [[Death/Legends|die]] when his plan backfired. ==Description== {{Quote|Sir! That thing's not just coming ''toward'' us! I-it's on a ''collision course! An Imperial probe droid!'' But it's bigger...'' better ''armed!''|A Rebel officer, seeing 13-K's approach|}} Modeled after the [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]], an exploration [[Probe droid/Legends|probot]] that was commonplace during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Infiltrator probe droid was augmented with superior [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating, powerful weaponry, and enhanced [[Computer/Legends|computer]] interface systems. Much larger than a Viper droid, the Infiltrator droid was packed with power, with forward [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] that were capable of blasting through a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette's]] [[Hull/Legends|hull]] from the outside, and it was an unrelenting and methodical killer in a combat situation. However, the Infiltrator droid's most crucial capabilities were its ability to plug into and control almost any electronic system, including large [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. An infiltrator droid had the ability to completely override every system it came into contact with, essentially making them an extension of itself. As such, it was able to function much more independently than most other probe droids, although Infiltrators could struggle to integrate its augmented programming with its base programming and thus suffer issues with its [[logic capacitator]]s. ==History== {{Quote|It's probe 13-K, sir. One of the models you had specially ''augmented'' by technological section."
"Of ''course'' it is. I wouldn't have exceeded orders by tampering with our probe unless I was ''certain'' of worthwhile results.|An Imperial officer and Admiral Damon Krell|}} The Infiltrator probe droid was produced during the height of the Galactic Civil War, as part of a joint venture between [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] under the oversight of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Damon Krell]]. In the wake of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Krell made the Infiltrator droid the central point of a plot to wipe out the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], and make himself the hero of the war. Krell dispatched an Infiltrator droid designated [[13-K]] to capture a Rebel blockade runner, commandeer the ship, and detonate it at the Rebel [[Haven (rendezvous point)|rendezvous point]]. However, the interference of Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] led to the plan's failure, with 13-K instead destroying Krell's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Infiltrator probe droid first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 45|''Star Wars'' (1977) 45]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1980]]. The droid was first identified as an Infiltrator probe droid in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', released in [[2006]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 45|''Star Wars'' (1977) 45]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 45}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{1stID|Infiltrator probe droid}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Arakyd Industries products]] [[Category:Combat probe droid models]] [[Category:Droid models of the Galactic Empire]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Infiltrator probe droids]] }}
